skill.name: sendgrid-automation
skill.description: Automate SendGrid email workflows through Rube MCP, including campaigns, contacts, sender identities, suppressions, and delivery analytics.
skill.summary: Guide agents through SendGrid campaign, contact, sender, suppression, and analytics workflows using Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many shell backtick, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are Markdown inline code spans and descriptive SendGrid terminology, not executable code. The confirmed risk is operational: the skill uses an external MCP endpoint and guides high-impact SendGrid actions such as sending campaigns, modifying contacts, deleting lists, and exporting activity data.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Network Connection
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs users to add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server and authenticate SendGrid through Rube. This is legitimate setup, but it depends on an external service and sends requests through a third-party MCP provider.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external endpoint and SendGrid authentication flow are stated directly in the setup section. The risk is third-party network dependency, not hidden exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: High-Impact SendGrid Account Operations
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The workflow guides agents to create and send campaigns, update contacts, manage sender identities, and delete lists. These are intended capabilities, but misuse can cause unwanted email sends, account changes, or irreversible list removal.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced workflows explicitly list campaign sending, contact changes, sender management, and list deletion tools. The text also warns that list deletion is irreversible.

medium_findings.title: Recipient Data and Activity Export Exposure
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill includes contact lookup, message activity filtering, CSV export requests, and CSV downloads. These authorized workflows can expose recipient personal data and message activity if used without proper access controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The SendGrid tools named in the skill directly cover contact lookup and activity CSV export. The exact data returned depends on the connected SendGrid account permissions.

low_findings.title: Static Shell Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The repeated Ruby or shell backtick findings refer to Markdown inline code spans around MCP tool names and parameter names. No evidence found of executable Ruby, shell command substitution, or user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation and the flagged backticks wrap tool names or parameters. There are no script blocks or executable language constructs in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Findings Lack Code Evidence
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance findings map to descriptive text, front matter, or parameter examples. No evidence found of hashing code, cryptographic APIs, host enumeration, or system probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain a description, SendGrid parameter guidance, or HTTP rate-limit guidance. They do not contain cryptographic or reconnaissance logic.

content.value_statement: SendGrid workflows often require many precise tool calls and account checks. This skill provides step-by-step guidance for campaigns, contacts, senders, suppressions, and analytics through Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Search current Rube MCP tool schemas before selecting SendGrid tools.
Create Single Send campaigns with sender, content, recipients, and unsubscribe settings.
Create lists and add or update contacts through SendGrid marketing tools.
Manage sender identities, verified senders, and domain authentication workflows.
Retrieve delivery statistics, category metrics, message activity, and CSV exports.
Review suppression groups and email-specific suppression status.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active authorized SendGrid connection.
Does not provide SendGrid credentials or bypass account permissions.
Cannot guarantee delivery, inbox placement, rate limits, or paid add-on access.
Destructive actions still require explicit user confirmation and account permission.

use_cases.title: Launch a Marketing Campaign
use_cases.description: Prepare a Single Send campaign by resolving lists, choosing a verified sender, and setting unsubscribe compliance fields.

use_cases.title: Maintain Contact Lists
use_cases.description: Create lists, add or update contacts, verify async jobs, and remove contacts from lists when needed.

use_cases.title: Review Email Performance
use_cases.description: Retrieve delivery metrics, category statistics, activity records, and CSV exports for reporting.

prompt_templates.title: Check SendGrid Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether Rube MCP is available, confirm the SendGrid connection status, and tell me what authorization step is needed next.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Contact List
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a SendGrid marketing list named Customer Webinar Leads, add these approved contacts, and verify the final contact count after processing.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Campaign
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Single Send draft for the Product Update list using our verified sender, this subject, HTML content, plain text content, and unsubscribe group.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Campaign Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Review SendGrid activity for last week, summarize delivery, bounce, open, and click trends, then request a CSV export if the data volume is high.

output_examples.input: Set up a newsletter campaign for the March product update.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists available marketing lists, selects a verified sender, checks unsubscribe settings, creates the campaign draft, and reports the created campaign name.

output_examples.input: Add webinar registrants to a new SendGrid list.
output_examples.output: The assistant creates the list, submits the contact upsert job, waits for processing, checks the list count, and flags any rejected contacts.

output_examples.input: Show email performance for the last 30 days.
output_examples.output: The assistant retrieves global statistics, summarizes delivery and engagement trends, notes paid add-on limits, and offers a CSV export for deeper analysis.

best_practices: Search Rube tool schemas before each workflow because MCP tool inputs can change.
Resolve names to IDs before sending campaigns, updating contacts, or changing sender settings.
Require explicit confirmation before sending emails, deleting lists, or exporting recipient activity.
anti_patterns: Do not send campaigns to all contacts unless the user explicitly approves the audience.
Do not mix legacy Contact Database IDs with Marketing API list and contact IDs.
Do not assume contact upsert jobs are complete until verification tools confirm results.

faq.question: Does this skill send emails directly?
faq.answer: It guides an agent to use authorized SendGrid tools through Rube MCP. The connected SendGrid account performs the actual send.

faq.question: What access is required?
faq.answer: Users need Rube MCP configured and an active SendGrid connection with permissions for the requested workflow.

faq.question: Can it manage contact lists?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers creating lists, adding or updating contacts, looking up contacts, and removing contacts from lists.

faq.question: Can it review analytics?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes global statistics, category metrics, message activity searches, and CSV export workflows.

faq.question: Does it handle compliance requirements?
faq.answer: It reminds users to include unsubscribe settings and verified senders, but compliance responsibility remains with the account owner.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill has no executable malware evidence, but it can trigger high-impact email, contact, sender, and export operations through a third-party MCP service.
